label: Reparation och underhåll av datorer, hushållsartiklar och varor för personligt bruk samt motorfordon och motorcyklar
description: Branschspecifik scaffold för verksamheter inom SNI 95 med fokus på felsökning, reparation, service, prisinformation och kontakt. Passar verkstäder och serviceföretag som lagar datorer, telefoner, hushållsapparater, cyklar, skor, klockor eller andra personliga brukssaker, samt aktörer som vill lyfta snabb återkoppling, garanti och tydliga kontaktvägar.
primaryJobs: Förklara vilka typer av reparationer och servicearbeten företaget utför
Bygga förtroende kring felsökning, garanti, reservdelar och arbetsprocess
Göra det enkelt att kontakta företaget för bokning, offert eller inlämning
Visa prisexempel eller startpriser för vanliga reparationer
Besvara vanliga frågor om felsökningstid, inlämning, garanti och reservdelar

routes.purpose: Positionera verksamheten, visa vilka produkter eller fordon som repareras och driva kontakt eller bokningsförfrågan.
Beskriva reparationstjänster, serviceområden och vanliga fel som företaget hjälper kunder med.
Ge tydlig prisinformation med exempel, startpriser eller felsökningsavgifter för att minska osäkerhet innan kontakt.
Konvertera besökare till kontakt med telefon, e-post, adress, öppettider och formulär för ärenden eller frågor.

routes.when: när företaget har en tydlig verkstadsprofil, historia, specialistkompetens eller team som stärker förtroendet
när många kunder behöver praktisk information om inlämning, felsökning, garantier, reservdelar eller ledtider

sectionOrderRules: Hero must state what the business repairs or services and include a primary contact CTA.
Service-summary should group concrete repair categories or common problem types rather than vague claims.
Repair-process should explain how inquiry, diagnosis, approval and handover work in clear steps.
Trust-proof must stay factual and may mention guarantees, original parts when actually used, and fast response when supported by the business data.
Opening-hours and map should appear near the lower part of the page unless contact details are central to the conversion goal.
Sections must avoid promising exact repair times or outcomes unless the business data supports it.
Services-intro must connect the business type to the specific repair work offered.
Service-list items must be concrete, for example screen replacement, battery change, diagnostics, sewing machine service, bicycle repair or appliance troubleshooting where relevant to the business.
Device-or-item-coverage should clarify which brands, product types, vehicles or item categories are accepted when such scope exists.
If both repair-process and pricing-highlight are shown, process should come before pricing to explain what is included in the work.
Pricing-intro must explain whether prices are fixed examples, starting prices, hourly rates or diagnosis fees.
Pricing-table must avoid invented package names and should use plain service labels customers recognize.
Pricing-notes must clarify what can affect final price, such as parts, fault complexity or customer approval after diagnosis.
Trust-proof on this page should support clarity and reassurance, not repeat the entire home page.
Contact-info must include real phone, email and address when available in the business data.
Opening-hours should be shown when provided and must not be replaced with placeholder text.
If map is present, it should follow contact-info or opening-hours and support visits or inlämning.
Contact-cta should support service inquiry, booking request or question submission without making unsupported promises.

mustPass: Page set reflects actual needs of repair and maintenance businesses by including services and pricing surfaces.
Every default route has a matching sectionsPerRoute entry.
All route ids, paths and section ids use kebab-case slugs.
Visitor-facing copy stays honest and avoids unsupported certifications, counts or guarantees.
All routes from routes.json are present.
No lorem ipsum or sample placeholder copy.
Generated code typechecks and the project builds.
avoid: Generic agency-style sections that do not fit a repair business.
Portfolio-first structure without explaining service categories or repair scope.
Vague service labels like 'high quality solutions' instead of actual repair work.
Hidden pricing structure when the site includes a dedicated pricing page.
Repeating the same trust claim in hero, trust-proof, reviews and CTA without adding new information.

compatibleDossiers.comment: Kandidat-rekommendationer härledda ur branschprofilen; verifiera mot riktiga Dossier-id före promotion.

selectionProfile.embeddingText: Scaffold for Swedish repair and maintenance businesses in SNI 95. Suitable for computer repair, phone repair, appliance repair, bicycle repair, shoe repair, watch service and similar workshops that need a trustworthy local business website with home, services, pricing and contact pages. Emphasizes diagnosis, repair categories, pricing transparency, guarantees, opening hours, workshop location and practical FAQ.
llmClassificationHints: Use this scaffold when the business primarily repairs, services or restores physical consumer items, electronics, household goods, bicycles, watches, shoes or similar goods.
Use this scaffold when customers need to understand what can be repaired, how the process works, what pricing looks like and how to contact the workshop.
Prefer this scaffold over a generic local service scaffold when pricing transparency and repair-process explanation are central to conversion.

variants.label: Draft Default
variants.description: Startvariant kopierad från referens-scaffoldens default så kandidaten kan förhandsgranskas; ersätts vid kurering.
